# Class: ContainerAccessor

Defined in: [integrations/docker/container-accessor.ts:32](https://github.com/jterrazz/package-test/blob/main/src/integrations/docker/container-accessor.ts#L32)

Assertion accessor for a single Docker container captured by the docker()
spec mode. Mirrors the shape of [CliResult](CliResult.md) so tests use the same
vocabulary (`stdout.toContain`, `file(...).content`, etc.) regardless
of where output came from.

Sync state (`exists`, `running`, `status`) is derived from the `docker
inspect` payload captured at result-construction time. Logs are fetched
lazily on first access to `stdout`/`stderr`.

## Constructors

```ts
new ContainerAccessor(
   containerId, 
   inspectData, 
   testDir, 
   transform?): ContainerAccessor;
```

## Accessors

### id

#### Get Signature

```ts
get id(): string | null;
```

Defined in: [integrations/docker/container-accessor.ts:48](https://github.com/jterrazz/package-test/blob/main/src/integrations/docker/container-accessor.ts#L48)

Underlying Docker container ID, or `null` if no container was captured
for this name. Useful when a follow-up CLI command needs to reference
the container by id (e.g. `spwn world inspect <id>`). Prefer the other
accessors for common reads — pulling the raw id is the escape hatch.

##### Returns

`string` \| `null`

***

### inspect

#### Get Signature

```ts
get inspect(): JsonAccessor;
```

Defined in: [integrations/docker/container-accessor.ts:77](https://github.com/jterrazz/package-test/blob/main/src/integrations/docker/container-accessor.ts#L77)

Raw `docker inspect` object for this container. Throws if the container
was not captured (i.e. `.exists === false`).

##### Returns

[`JsonAccessor`](JsonAccessor.md)

***

### file()

```ts
file(path): FileAccessor;
```

Defined in: [integrations/docker/container-accessor.ts:103](https://github.com/jterrazz/package-test/blob/main/src/integrations/docker/container-accessor.ts#L103)

Read a file from inside the container via `docker exec cat`. The
returned object satisfies the same [FileAccessor](../interfaces/FileAccessor.md) shape used by
the host filesystem accessor, so tests do not need to learn a new API.

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `path` | `string` |

#### Returns

[`FileAccessor`](../interfaces/FileAccessor.md)
